_README: Curated scout-speak trait phrases for the Deep Dive form. Click an item in the picker to add it to Strengths / Weaknesses / Swing Factors instead of typing. Polarity: positive=strength, negative=weakness, swing=swing factor / translation question. Positions: G=guard, W=wing, F=forward, B=big, ALL=any. Edit this file to add/remove phrases — picker updates on next deploy.

items.text: Live-dribble pull-up creates separation against larger defenders
Downhill burst out of the pick-and-roll
Shifty handle in tight spaces with directional change
Step-back jumper used as a true counter, not a bail-out
Pace control — knows when to probe vs reset
Hesitation move freezes defenders at the nail
Self-creation works at all three levels
Comfortable scoring in iso vs switches
Limited self-creation against set defenses
Right-hand dominant — defenders steer him left
Footwork breaks down under live ball pressure
Settles for tough jumpers instead of attacking the rim
Plays at one speed — predictable for primary defenders
Three-level scoring threat
Quick release out of the catch — minimal gather
Confident on movement triples — curls, pin-downs, hand-offs
Pull-up range extends well beyond the arc
Free throw mechanics support the 3-point projection
Floater works in the in-between space
Tough shot-maker — high success rate on contested looks
Three-point gravity already pulls help defenders early
Inconsistent base — feet drift on contested attempts
Slow release creates contest opportunities
Free throw mechanics raise translation concerns
Mid-range volume hasn't held against length
Catch-and-shoot only — no pull-up game on tape yet
Limited 3-point sample size at meaningful difficulty
Vertical pop and touch at the rim through contact
Crafty finishes around length using both hands
Two-foot finisher — absorbs contact and finishes through
Strong second-jump finisher around the basket
High free throw rate — lives at the line
Wide finishing package — Eurosteps, scoops, lefty hooks
Below-rim finisher — relies on craft, not athleticism
Off-hand finishing limited
Avoids contact at the rim
Low free throw rate — doesn't draw fouls
Live-dribble passing — creates for others without giving up his attack
Reads pick-and-roll coverages — drop, hedge, switch — accurately
Drive-and-kick precision creates open looks
Hits the second-side weak-side shooter consistently
Throws the lob with anticipation, not reaction
Manipulates the second defender to free shooters
Strong assist-to-turnover ratio under pressure
Connecting passer on quick swings
Tunnel vision after the first read closes
Turnover-prone against aggressive on-ball pressure
Telegraphs passes — defenders break to the receiver
Limited as a primary creator — better as a connector
Doesn't manipulate ball-screen reads — takes the first available
Smart cutter — relocates to open windows
Hits relocation 3s after handoff actions
Active off-ball — creates passing windows for the ball-handler
Sets quality screens and flips into actions
Effective short-roll passer
Spaces the floor with respected gravity even when not shooting volume
Stationary off-ball — doesn't create passing lanes
Standstill spot-up only — limited movement value
Doesn't read cuts vs sealing big defenders
Screens are soft — defenders don't get caught
Switchable across 1-3 with light feet and length
Switchable across 1-4 — guards the action all over the floor
POA defender — picks up full court
Strong on-ball discipline — doesn't reach
Quick lateral slides cover ground without crossing feet
Locks in on stars — accepts the assignment
Wrestles guards at the point of attack with active hands
Stays attached on movement — fights through screens
Loses shooters off screens — late on closeouts
Blown by shot-creators with first-step speed
Hip stiffness shows on directional changes
Position-locked — likely tied to one matchup at the next level
Reaches in on drives — fouls instead of contesting verticality
Bites on pump fakes and ball-fakes consistently
Strong help-side rotator — reads the ball
Plus low-man — stops the lob and tags the roller
Disrupts passing lanes — generates events
Weak-side shot-blocker — protects the rim from help
Communicates coverages pre-action
Closes out with discipline — sprints, gathers, contests
Drop coverage anchor — vertical contests at the rim
Switch-and-recover instincts — doesn't lose his man
Reactive help-defender — late on rotations
Lapses in transition — slow getting back
Doesn't tag the roller — vulnerable on lobs
Soft drop — invites layups rather than verticality
Closeouts come up short or out of control
Active offensive rebounder — second-jump tap-outs
Boxes out smaller players consistently
Rebounds out of his area — chases long boards
Plus defensive rebounder — finishes possessions
Guard-rebounder — initiates breaks off the board
Below-position-average rebounder
Gets pushed off his spot by stronger players
Doesn't seal — gives up second-chance opportunities
Vertical pop at the rim — finishes above defenders
Length plays bigger than measurements
Burst in the open floor — leaks out for transition buckets
Stop-and-go burst opens driving lanes
Lateral quickness translates to defensive versatility
Recovery speed — closes ground from late angles
Average athlete by NBA standards
Heavy-footed in space — limited as a switcher
Limited explosion off two feet
One-speed athlete — no separation gear
NBA-ready frame — won't be overpowered Year 1
Long arms relative to height — broken-shape defense
Strong core — holds position vs bigger players
Frame projects to add functional weight without losing quickness
Hand size + length combo creates deflection events
Slight build — needs to add 12-18 lbs of functional weight
Short wingspan limits defensive ceiling
Lower-body looks closer to ceiling than baseline
Can be moved off his spot by NBA strength
High motor — plays at one speed all 40 minutes
Best player in the 4th quarter — clutch by makeup
Coachable — accepts feedback in real time
Vocal leader — organizes coverages and calls actions
Bounces back from mistakes without dragging the next play
Wired competitor — takes possessions personally
Plays through fatigue — late-game effort is the same as Q1
Effort wavers when shots aren't falling
Disengaged when not the primary scorer
Body language slips after mistakes
Transition motor — slow to sprint back
Reads coverages pre-snap — adjusts without the bench prompting
Quick processor — sees the play two actions ahead
Plays the right read — doesn't hunt for personal offense
Knows where his scoring comes from — high-percentage shot diet
Manipulates rotations on offense — uses the second defender's reactions
Defensive recognition — calls out screens and tags rollers proactively
Slow defensive recognition — late on rotations
Plays in the present — doesn't read sequences
Forces shots that don't fit the offense
Reactive vs proactive — defenders dictate the read
Skill set scales up or down — plays on or off ball
Wins with feel and skill — not dependent on physical advantage
Defensive identity translates without forcing specialization
Free-throw indicator backs up the 3-point projection
Profile fits a clear NBA archetype — no forcing the role
Role-locked — needs the right system to thrive
Wins on physical advantage that won't carry to the NBA
Volume scoring relied on shot diet that won't survive scouting
Defensive ceiling tied to a single coverage
Injury history (ankle/knee/back)
Maturity / off-court question marks
Limited tape against high-major or pro competition
Older prospect — limited remaining development runway
Conditioning concerns — can he handle the 82-game grind
Frame may not add functional weight without losing quickness
Shooting mechanics inconsistent enough to question the projection
Defensive engagement waxes and wanes within games
Reach at draft slot — some boards have him 15+ picks lower

Does the assist rate hold against length and switching?

Can he stay on the floor in playoff matchups, or does he get hunted?

Does the defensive intensity hold without ball-dominance?
Can the handle survive NBA-level on-ball pressure?
Will the rim-protection number translate without scheme support?
Does the off-ball gravity grow as the shot becomes respected?

Will the touch around the rim translate vs longer defenders?
Can he avoid foul trouble defending bigger NBA frames?
